### Starting a Workflow with Latest Deployment

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/workflow-api/start.mdx

This example demonstrates how to use the `start` function to initiate a workflow run and specifies `deploymentId: "latest"` to target the most recent deployment for the current environment.

```APIDOC
## Starting a Workflow with Latest Deployment

### Description
Initiates a workflow run, targeting the latest deployment for the current environment.

### Method
`start(workflow, args, options)`

### Parameters
#### Arguments
- `workflow`: The workflow function to execute.
- `args`: An array of arguments to pass to the workflow.
- `options`: An object containing configuration options.
  - `deploymentId` (string): Set to `"latest"` to automatically resolve the most recent deployment for the current environment.

### Request Example
```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { myWorkflow } from "./workflows/my-workflow";

const run = await start(myWorkflow, ["arg1", "arg2"], {
  deploymentId: "latest"
});
```

### Notes
- Using `deploymentId: "latest"` means the workflow run might execute on a different deployment than the one calling `start()`.
- Ensure workflow identity, input, and output compatibility across deployments when using this option.
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Run Published @workflow/web Package

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/web/README.md

Installs the @workflow/web package and its React dependencies, then starts the Next.js server from the installed package. Ensure React runtime dependencies are provided by the host project. The PORT environment variable can be used to specify the port.

```bash
mkdir workflow-observability-ui
cd workflow-observability-ui

npm init -y
# You must provide React runtime dependencies in your host project.
npm i @workflow/web react react-dom

# Run Next.js from the installed package (it contains the .next output)
cd node_modules/@workflow/web
npx --yes next start -p "${PORT:-3456}"
```

--------------------------------

### Hono Framework App Boundary Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/skills/migrating-to-workflow-sdk/references/runtime-targets.md

This example shows how to start a workflow within a Hono framework application. It defines a POST route to initiate the onboarding workflow.

```typescript
import { Hono } from 'hono';
import { start } from 'workflow/api';
import { onboardingWorkflow } from '../workflows/onboarding';

const app = new Hono();

app.post('/api/onboarding/start', async (c) => {
  const body = (await c.req.json()) as { userId: string };
  const run = await start(onboardingWorkflow, [body.userId]);
  return c.json({ runId: run.runId });
});

export default app;
```

--------------------------------

### Create TanStack Start Project

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/tanstack-start.mdx

Use the TanStack CLI to create a new project and install the workflow package.

```bash
npx @tanstack/cli create my-workflow-app
```

```bash
cd my-workflow-app
```

```bash
npm i workflow
```

--------------------------------

### Create SvelteKit Project

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/sveltekit.mdx

Use this command to create a new SvelteKit project with a minimal setup. Navigate into the created directory and install the workflow package.

```bash
npx sv create my-workflow-app --template=minimal --types=ts --no-add-ons
```

```bash
cd my-workflow-app
```

```bash
npm i workflow
```

--------------------------------

### Start Postgres World in SvelteKit

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/deploying/world/postgres-world.mdx

Create a src/hooks.server.ts file to start the world on server start.

```typescript
import type { ServerInit } from "@sveltejs/kit";

export const init: ServerInit = async () => {
  const { getWorld } = await import("workflow/runtime");
  const world = await getWorld();
  await world.start?.();
};
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Next.js Start Script

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/web/README.md

Sets a start script in the host package.json to run the Next.js server from the installed @workflow/web package, allowing port configuration via the PORT environment variable.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cd node_modules/@workflow/web && next start -p $PORT"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Start Production Server

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/workbench/tanstack-start/README.md

Commands to build the project for production and then start the production server.

```sh
pnpm build && pnpm start
```

--------------------------------

### Workflow SDK: Starting a Workflow Run

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/migration-guides/migrating-from-temporal.mdx

Replaces Temporal's Worker and Task Queue setup with a single `start()` call from an API route. This example initiates the `processOrder` workflow.

```typescript
import { start } from 'workflow/api';
import { processOrder } from '@/workflows/order';

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const { orderId } = (await request.json()) as { orderId: string };
  const run = await start(processOrder, [orderId]); // [!code highlight]
  return Response.json({ runId: run.runId });
}
```

--------------------------------

### start with StartOptions

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/workflow-api/start.mdx

Starts a new workflow run with arguments and StartOptions.

```APIDOC
## start with StartOptions

### Description
Starts a new workflow run with arguments and StartOptions.

### Method
`start`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { myWorkflow } from "./workflows/my-workflow";

const run = await start(myWorkflow, ["arg1", "arg2"], {
  deploymentId: "custom-deployment-id"
});
```
```

--------------------------------

### Start Postgres World in Next.js

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/deploying/world/postgres-world.mdx

Create an instrumentation.ts file in your project root to start the world on server start.

```typescript
export async function register() {
  if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME !== "edge") {
    const { getWorld } = await import("workflow/runtime");
    const world = await getWorld();
    await world.start?.();
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Framework-Agnostic App Boundary Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/skills/migrating-to-workflow-sdk/references/runtime-targets.md

This example demonstrates starting a workflow from a framework-agnostic API endpoint using plain Request and Response objects. It's suitable when a framework-agnostic approach is required, even if specific frameworks are mentioned elsewhere.

```typescript
import { start } from 'workflow/api';
import { onboardingWorkflow } from '../workflows/onboarding';

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const body = (await request.json()) as { userId: string };
  const run = await start(onboardingWorkflow, [body.userId]);
  return Response.json({ runId: run.runId });
}
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Tmux Session for Workflow SDK Development

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/skills/internal-dev-workbench/SKILL.md

This script initializes a 3-pane tmux session for Workflow SDK development. It configures each pane with specific commands for the Next.js workbench, observability UI, and a scratchpad shell, all managed by portless for worktree-scoped URLs. Ensure prerequisites like tmux and portless are installed, and the repository is bootstrapped.

```bash
REPO=/path/to/workflow--<worktree-suffix>
# Session name = basename of the branch (matches portless's subdomain prefix
# and the statusline's `tmux attach -t <prefix>` indicator). For branch
# `pgp/foo-bar` this resolves to `foo-bar`.
SESSION=$(git -C "$REPO" rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
SESSION="${SESSION##*/}"

# Create the session and capture the initial pane ID
PANE_DEV=$(tmux new-session -d -s "$SESSION" -c "$REPO" -P -F '#{pane_id}')
PANE_OBS=$(tmux split-window -h -t "$PANE_DEV" -c "$REPO" -P -F '#{pane_id}')
PANE_SH=$(tmux split-window -v -t "$PANE_OBS" -c "$REPO" -P -F '#{pane_id}')	mux select-layout -t "$SESSION" main-vertical

# Pane DEV (left): Next.js turbopack workbench, with manifest exposed for e2e
tmux send-keys -t "$PANE_DEV" \
  'cd workbench/nextjs-turbopack && WORKFLOW_PUBLIC_MANIFEST=1 portless run --name turbopack pnpm dev' C-m

# Pane OBS (top-right): observability UI scoped to the workbench app
tmux send-keys -t "$PANE_OBS" \
  'cd workbench/nextjs-turbopack && portless run --name workflow-obs sh -c "pnpm workflow web --webPort $PORT --noBrowser"' C-m

# Pane SH (bottom-right): scratchpad at repo root
tmux send-keys -t "$PANE_SH" 'echo "scratchpad: $(pwd)"' C-m

tmux attach -t "$SESSION"
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Flight Booking App Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/ai/index.mdx

Clone the Flight Booking Agent example repository to use as a starting point for building a durable AI agent. This app includes a Next.js chat interface, AI SDK, and Shadcn UI.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vercel/workflow-examples -b plain-ai-sdk
cd workflow-examples/flight-booking-app
```

--------------------------------

### Local Development Setup with Docker Compose

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/world-postgres/README.md

Set up your local development environment using Docker Compose for PostgreSQL. This includes starting the database, running migrations, and exporting necessary environment variables.

```bash
# Start PostgreSQL database
docker-compose up -d

# Create and run migrations
pnpm drizzle-kit generate
pnpm drizzle-kit migrate

# Set environment variables for local development
export WORKFLOW_POSTGRES_URL="postgres://world:world@localhost:5432/world"
export WORKFLOW_TARGET_WORLD="@workflow/world-postgres"
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Chat Setup Example

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/workflow-ai/workflow-chat-transport.mdx

Demonstrates how to integrate WorkflowChatTransport into a React component using the `useChat` hook from `@ai-sdk/react`.

```typescript
"use client";

import { useChat } from "@ai-sdk/react";
import { WorkflowChatTransport } from "@workflow/ai";
import { useState } from "react";

export default function BasicChat() {
  const [input, setInput] = useState("");
  const { messages, sendMessage } = useChat({
    transport: new WorkflowChatTransport(),
  });

  return (
    <div>
      <div className="space-y-4">
        {messages.map((m) => (
          <div key={m.id}>
            <strong>{m.role}:</strong> {m.content}
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>

      <form
        onSubmit={(e) => {
          e.preventDefault();
          sendMessage({ text: input });
          setInput("");
        }}
      >
        <input
          value={input}
          placeholder="Say something..."
          onChange={(e) => setInput(e.currentTarget.value)}
        />
      </form>
    </div>
  );
}

```

--------------------------------

### Install AI SDK v6

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/ai/README.md

Install the latest AI SDK v6, which is recommended.

```bash
npm install ai

### start with arguments

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/workflow-api/start.mdx

Starts a new workflow run with provided arguments.

```APIDOC
## start with arguments

### Description
Starts a new workflow run with provided arguments.

### Method
`start`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { userSignupWorkflow } from "./workflows/user-signup";

const run = await start(userSignupWorkflow, ["user@example.com"]);
```
```

--------------------------------

### Local E2E Testing Setup for Next.js Turbopack

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/AGENTS.md

Steps to set up and run end-to-end tests locally against a Next.js Turbopack application. Requires starting a dev server and then running tests.

```bash
# Step 1: Start the dev server in background
# NOTE: WORKFLOW_PUBLIC_MANIFEST=1 is required for e2e tests to access the workflow manifest
cd workbench/nextjs-turbopack && WORKFLOW_PUBLIC_MANIFEST=1 pnpm dev > /tmp/nextjs-dev.log 2>&1 &

# Step 2: Wait for server to be ready (usually 15-20 seconds)
sleep 15

# Step 3: Run the e2e tests from the project root
DEPLOYMENT_URL="http://localhost:3000" APP_NAME="nextjs-turbopack" pnpm vitest run packages/core/e2e/e2e.test.ts

# Step 4: Stop the dev server when done
pkill -f "pnpm dev"

# To run specific tests, use the -t flag:
DEPLOYMENT_URL="http://localhost:3000" APP_NAME="nextjs-turbopack" pnpm vitest run packages/core/e2e/e2e.test.ts -t "sleeping"
```

--------------------------------

### Start Development Server

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/astro.mdx

Run this command in your terminal to start the Astro development server.

```bash
npm run dev

```

### `start`

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/cookbook/advanced/publishing-libraries.mdx

Starts a workflow run.

```APIDOC
## `start`

### Description
Starts a workflow run.

### Usage
```javascript
import { start } from '@vercel/workflow'

await start('workflow-name', { input: 'data' })
```
```

Use this command to start the development server for the TanStack Start workbench.

```sh
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Expose HTTP Endpoints with Bun.serve()

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/how-it-works/framework-integrations.mdx

Wire up generated workflow handlers to HTTP endpoints using Bun.serve(). This example shows routing for flow, step, webhook, and a custom workflow start endpoint.

```typescript
import flow from "./.well-known/workflow/v1/flow.js";
import step from "./.well-known/workflow/v1/step.js";
import * as webhook from "./.well-known/workflow/v1/webhook.js";

import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { handleUserSignup } from "./workflows/user-signup.js";

const server = Bun.serve({
  port: process.env.PORT,
  routes: {
    "/.well-known/workflow/v1/flow": {
      POST: (req) => flow.POST(req),
    },
    "/.well-known/workflow/v1/step": {
      POST: (req) => step.POST(req),
    },
    // webhook exports handlers for GET, POST, DELETE, etc.
    "/.well-known/workflow/v1/webhook/:token": webhook,

    // Example: Start a workflow
    "/": {
      GET: async (req) => {
        const email = `test-${crypto.randomUUID()}@test.com`;
        const run = await start(handleUserSignup, [email]);
        return Response.json({
          message: "User signup workflow started",
          runId: run.runId,
        });
      },
    },
  },
});

console.log(`Server listening on http://localhost:${server.port}`);

```

--------------------------------

### Vitest Global Setup Script for Dev Server

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/testing/server-based.mdx

This script starts a dev server using Nitro before tests and tears it down afterwards. It logs server events and sets the WORKFLOW_LOCAL_BASE_URL environment variable. Use this for server-based testing scenarios.

```typescript
import { spawn } from "node:child_process";
import { setTimeout as delay } from "node:timers/promises";
import type { ChildProcess } from "node:child_process";

let server: ChildProcess | null = null;
const PORT = "4000";

function emitSetupLog(event: string, fields: Record<string, unknown> = {}) {
  console.log(
    JSON.stringify({
      scope: "workflow-server-test",
      event,
      port: PORT,
      ...fields,
    })
  );
}

export async function setup() { // [!code highlight]
  const stdout: string[] = [];
  const stderr: string[] = [];

  emitSetupLog("server_starting", {
    command: `npx nitro dev --port ${PORT}`,
  });

  server = spawn("npx", ["nitro", "dev", "--port", PORT], {
    stdio: "pipe",
    detached: false,
    env: process.env,
  });

  const ready = await new Promise<boolean>((resolve) => {
    const timeout = setTimeout(() => resolve(false), 15_000);

    server?.stdout?.on("data", (data) => {
      const output = data.toString();
      stdout.push(output);
      emitSetupLog("server_stdout", { message: output.trim() });

      if (output.includes("listening") || output.includes("ready")) {
        clearTimeout(timeout);
        resolve(true);
      }
    });

    server?.stderr?.on("data", (data) => {
      const output = data.toString();
      stderr.push(output);
      emitSetupLog("server_stderr", { message: output.trim() });
    });

    server?.on("error", (error) => {
      emitSetupLog("server_process_error", {
        name: error.name,
        message: error.message,
      });
      clearTimeout(timeout);
      resolve(false);
    });

    server?.on("exit", (code, signal) => {
      emitSetupLog("server_exit", { code, signal });
    });
  });

  if (!ready) {
    const recentStdout = stdout.join("").trim().slice(-2000);
    const recentStderr = stderr.join("").trim().slice(-2000);

    throw new Error(
      [
        "Server failed to start within 15 seconds.",
        `Command: npx nitro dev --port ${PORT}`,
        `WORKFLOW_LOCAL_BASE_URL: http://localhost:${PORT}`,
        `Recent stdout:\n${recentStdout || "(empty)"}`,
        `Recent stderr:\n${recentStderr || "(empty)"}`,
      ].join("\n\n")
    );
  }

  await delay(2_000);

  process.env.WORKFLOW_LOCAL_BASE_URL = `http://localhost:${PORT}`; // [!code highlight]

  emitSetupLog("server_ready", {
    baseUrl: process.env.WORKFLOW_LOCAL_BASE_URL,
  });
}

export async function teardown() { // [!code highlight]
  if (!server) return;

  emitSetupLog("server_stopping");

  server.kill("SIGTERM");
  await delay(1_000);

  if (!server.killed) {
    emitSetupLog("server_force_kill");
    server.kill("SIGKILL");
  }
}

```

--------------------------------

### Start Postgres World in Nitro (Plugin)

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/deploying/world/postgres-world.mdx

Create a plugin to start the world on server initialization.

```typescript
import { defineNitroPlugin } from "nitro/~internal/runtime/plugin";

export default defineNitroPlugin(async () => {
  const { getWorld } = await import("workflow/runtime");
  const world = await getWorld();
  await world.start?.();
});
```

--------------------------------

### Install @workflow/world-postgres

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/world-postgres/README.md

Install the package using npm, pnpm, or yarn.

```bash
npm install @workflow/world-postgres
# or
pnpm add @workflow/world-postgres
# or
yarn add @workflow/world-postgres
```

### Install Workflow SDK

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/migration-guides/migrating-from-inngest.mdx

Install the Workflow SDK using pnpm. Framework integrations are subpath exports of the same package.

```bash
pnpm add workflow
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Run Workflow CLI

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/observability/index.mdx

Installs and runs the Workflow CLI temporarily if not already installed. Use this to quickly access CLI commands.

```bash
npx workflow
```

### Create Express Project and Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/express.mdx

Commands to create a new Express project, initialize npm, and install necessary packages including workflow, express, nitro, and rollup. If using TypeScript, also install @types/express.

```bash
mkdir my-workflow-app
```

```bash
cd my-workflow-app
```

```bash
npm init --y
```

```bash
npm i workflow express nitro rollup
```

```bash
npm i -D @types/express
```

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/fastify.mdx

Install the necessary packages for workflow, Fastify, and Nitro.

```bash
npm i workflow fastify nitro rollup
```

```bash
pip install vercel

### Install Workflow SDK Migration Skill

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/migration-guides/index.mdx

Use this command to install the migration skill for the Workflow SDK.

```bash
npx skills add https://github.com/vercel/workflow --skill migrating-to-workflow-sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Start a Workflow Run

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/migration-guides/migrating-from-inngest.mdx

Initiate workflows directly using `start()` instead of `inngest.send()`. This eliminates the need for an event bus and registry.

```typescript
import { start } from 'workflow/api';
import { processOrder } from '@/workflows/order';

const run = await start(processOrder, [orderId]); // [!code highlight]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Workflow, Nitro, and Rollup Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/hono.mdx

Install the necessary npm packages for workflow management, build system, and bundling.

```bash
npm i workflow nitro rollup
```

--------------------------------

### Workflow SDK: Spawn Child Workflow

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/migration-guides/migrating-from-temporal.mdx

In Workflow SDK v5, child workflows are spawned by calling `start()` directly from the parent workflow. This example starts a `childWorkflow`.

```typescript
import { start } from 'workflow/api';

export async function parentWorkflow(item: string) {
  'use workflow';
  const child = await start(childWorkflow, [item]); // [!code highlight]
  return { childRunId: child.runId };
}
```

--------------------------------

### Setup PostgreSQL Database with CLI

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/world-postgres/README.md

Use the CLI tool to automatically set up your PostgreSQL database. It loads .env files and prioritizes WORKFLOW_POSTGRES_URL, then DATABASE_URL, or defaults to a local instance.

```bash
pnpm exec workflow-postgres-setup
# or
npm exec workflow-postgres-setup
```

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/astro.mdx

Change into the newly created Astro project directory.

```bash
cd my-workflow-app
```

```bash
mkdir my-workflow-app

```bash

```bash
npm init --y

--------------------------------

### start()

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/workflow-api/index.mdx

Start/enqueue a new workflow run.

```APIDOC
## start()

### Description
Start/enqueue a new workflow run.

### Method
Not specified (likely a function call within a runtime environment)

### Parameters
Not specified in the source text.

### Request Example
Not specified in the source text.

### Response
Not specified in the source text.
```

--------------------------------

### Temporal Workflow Activity Proxy

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/migration-guides/migrating-from-temporal.mdx

In Temporal, activities are proxied through a module. This example shows the setup for proxying the `chargePayment` activity.

```typescript
import * as wf from '@temporalio/workflow';
import type * as activities from './activities';

const { chargePayment } = wf.proxyActivities<typeof activities>({
  startToCloseTimeout: '5 minutes',
});

export async function processOrder(orderId: string) {
  await chargePayment(orderId);
  return { orderId, status: 'completed' };
}
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Use Workflow CLI for Example App

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/CLAUDE.md

Commands to build workflow bundles for the example app and use the workflow CLI directly. The 'wf' command is a shorthand for 'workflow'.

```bash
# Build workflow bundles for example app
cd workbench/example && pnpm build

# Use workflow CLI directly
cd workbench/example && pnpm workflow [command]
cd workbench/example && pnpm wf [command]  # shorthand
```

--------------------------------

### Create API Route Handler for Workflow

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/vite.mdx

Define a POST API route handler to invoke your workflow. This example shows how to parse JSON input and asynchronously start a workflow.

```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { defineEventHandler } from "nitro/h3";
import { handleUserSignup } from "../workflows/user-signup";

export default defineEventHandler(async ({ req }) => {
  const { email } = await req.json() as { email: string };
  // Executes asynchronously and doesn't block your app
  await start(handleUserSignup, [email]);
  return {
    message: "User signup workflow started",
  };
});
```

--------------------------------

### Stream Chat Messages

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/ai/chat-session-modeling.mdx

Handles GET requests to stream chat messages for a given workflow run ID. It retrieves the run and its readable stream, optionally starting from a specific index.

```typescript
import { createUIMessageStreamResponse } from "ai";
import { getRun } from "workflow/api";

export async function GET(
  request: Request,
  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }
) {
  const { id } = await params;
  const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
  const startIndex = searchParams.get("startIndex");

  const run = getRun(id); // [!code highlight]
  const stream = run.getReadable({ // [!code highlight]
    startIndex: startIndex ? parseInt(startIndex, 10) : undefined, // [!code highlight]
  }); // [!code highlight]

  return createUIMessageStreamResponse({ stream });
}
```

--------------------------------

### Enter Pre-release Mode

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/CLAUDE.md

Enter pre-release mode for the changesets tool. Use 'beta' for beta versions or 'rc' for release candidates.

```bash
pnpm changeset pre enter beta
```

```bash
pnpm changeset pre enter rc
```

--------------------------------

### Consume Namespaced Streams in an API

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/foundations/streaming.mdx

Access specific namespaced streams using `run.getReadable` within an API route to retrieve data. This example shows how to get the logs stream and return it to the client.

```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { multiStreamWorkflow } from "./workflows/multi";

type LogEntry = { level: string; message: string };
type MetricEntry = { cpu: number; memory: number };

export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const run = await start(multiStreamWorkflow);

  // Access specific named streams // [!code highlight]
  const logs = run.getReadable<LogEntry>({ namespace: "logs" }); // [!code highlight]
  const metrics = run.getReadable<MetricEntry>({ namespace: "metrics" }); // [!code highlight]

  // Return the logs stream to the client
  return new Response(logs, {
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
  });
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create NestJS Project and Install Workflow SDK

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/nestjs.mdx

Use the NestJS CLI to create a new project and then install the workflow and @workflow/nest packages.

```bash
npm i -g @nestjs/cli
nest new my-workflow-app
```

```bash
cd my-workflow-app
```

```bash
npm i workflow @workflow/nest
```

--------------------------------

### Access Run Object Properties

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/foundations/starting-workflows.mdx

The Run object returned by start() provides access to the workflow's status and results. Await async getters like status and returnValue to get their values.

```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { processOrder } from "./workflows/process-order";

const run = await start(processOrder, [/* orderId */]);

// The run object has properties you can await
console.log("Run ID:", run.runId);

// Check the workflow status
const status = await run.status; // "running" | "completed" | "failed"

// Get the workflow's return value (blocks until completion)
const result = await run.returnValue;
```

--------------------------------

### PostCSS Configuration for Tailwind CSS

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/web-shared/README.md

Example configuration for postcss.config.mjs in a Next.js application to ensure Tailwind CSS classes are correctly processed by PostCSS. This setup is necessary for the styling of components from '@workflow/web-shared' to be applied correctly.

```javascript
const config = {
  plugins: ['@tailwindcss/postcss'],
};

export default config;
```

--------------------------------

### Create Hono Route Handler for Workflow

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/getting-started/hono.mdx

Create a Hono API route handler to trigger a workflow. This example sets up a POST endpoint that accepts an email, starts the user signup workflow, and returns a confirmation message.

```typescript
import { Hono } from "hono";
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { handleUserSignup } from "../workflows/user-signup.js";

const app = new Hono();

app.post("/api/signup", async (c) => {
  const { email } = await c.req.json();
  await start(handleUserSignup, [email]);
  return c.json({ message: "User signup workflow started" });
});

export default app;
```

--------------------------------

### setupWorkflowTests()

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/vitest/index.mdx

Sets up an in-process workflow runtime in each test worker. Imports pre-built bundles, creates a Local World instance with direct handlers, and sets it as the global world. Clears all workflow data on each invocation for full test isolation. Called automatically by the `workflow()` plugin in `setupFiles`. Use directly only for manual setup.

```APIDOC
## setupWorkflowTests()

### Description
Sets up an in-process workflow runtime in each test worker. Imports pre-built bundles, creates a Local World instance with direct handlers, and sets it as the global world. Clears all workflow data on each invocation for full test isolation. Called automatically by the `workflow()` plugin in `setupFiles`. Use directly only for manual setup.

### Method
`setupWorkflowTests()`

### Parameters
#### Options
- **options?** (`WorkflowTestOptions`) - Optional - Optional configuration

### Returns
`Promise<void>`
```

--------------------------------

### Direct API Calls with Workflow Fetch

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/errors/fetch-in-workflow.mdx

This example demonstrates how to use the fetch step function directly for making custom HTTP requests to external APIs within a workflow. It shows a basic GET request and JSON parsing.

```typescript
import { fetch } from "workflow";

export async function dataWorkflow() {
  "use workflow";

  // Use fetch directly for HTTP requests
  const response = await fetch("https://api.example.com/data"); // [!code highlight] 
  const data = await response.json();

  return data;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Build and run for Node.js

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/workbench/express/README.md

Compiles the project and executes the generated server entry point.

```sh
npm run build
node .output/server/index.mjs
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Usage

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/workflow-next/with-workflow.mdx

Wrap your `nextConfig` with `withWorkflow` to enable `"use step"` and `"use workflow"` directives.

```APIDOC
## Basic Usage

### Description
Wrap your `nextConfig` with `withWorkflow` to enable `"use step"` and `"use workflow"` directives while developing locally or deploying to production.

### Method
`withWorkflow` is a function that takes your Next.js configuration object and an optional configuration object for workflow options.

### Parameters
#### `nextConfig` (NextConfig) - Required
Your existing Next.js configuration object.

#### `workflowConfig` (object) - Optional
An object to configure workflow options. See the 'Options' section for details.

### Request Example
```typescript
import { withWorkflow } from "workflow/next";
import type { NextConfig } from "next";

const nextConfig: NextConfig = {
  // ... rest of your Next.js config
};

const workflowConfig = {}; // Optional configuration

export default withWorkflow(nextConfig, workflowConfig);
```

### Response
Returns a modified Next.js configuration object with workflow transformations enabled.
```

--------------------------------

### Start a Child Workflow from a Step Function

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/skills/workflow/SKILL.md

To start child workflows from within a parent workflow, wrap the start() call in a step function. This ensures proper execution context. The start() function returns immediately.

```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";

// Wrap start() in a step function
async function triggerChild(data: string) {
  "use step";
  const run = await start(childWorkflow, [data]);
  return run.runId;
}

export async function parentWorkflow() {
  "use workflow";
  const childRunId = await triggerChild("some data");  // Fire-and-forget via step
  await sleep("1h");
}
```

--------------------------------

### Pass Workflow Function Directly to start()

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/errors/start-invalid-workflow-function.mdx

Pass the imported workflow function directly to `start()`, not a wrapper callback. Arguments should be provided in the second parameter of `start()`.

```typescript
import { start } from "workflow/api";
import { sendReminder } from "./workflows/send-reminder";

export async function POST() {
  await start(sendReminder, ["hello@example.com"]); // [!code highlight]
}
```

--------------------------------

### buildWorkflowTests()

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/api-reference/vitest/index.mdx

Builds workflow and step bundles to disk. Called automatically by the `workflow()` plugin in `globalSetup`. Use directly only for manual setup.

```APIDOC
## buildWorkflowTests()

### Description
Builds workflow and step bundles to disk. Called automatically by the `workflow()` plugin in `globalSetup`. Use directly only for manual setup.

### Method
`buildWorkflowTests()`

### Parameters
#### Options
- **options?** (`WorkflowTestOptions`) - Optional - Optional configuration

### Returns
`Promise<void>`
```

--------------------------------

### Install @workflow/ai

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/packages/ai/README.md

Install the @workflow/ai package using npm.

```bash
npm install @workflow/ai
```

--------------------------------

### Enter Pre-release Mode

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/AGENTS.md

Initiates pre-release mode for Changesets, typically used for publishing beta versions. Specify the pre-release type (e.g., 'beta').

```bash
pnpm changeset pre enter beta
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Preview Production App

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/workbench/sveltekit/README.md

Compiles the application into a production-ready build and provides a command to preview the generated output locally.

```shell
npm run build

npm run preview
```

--------------------------------

### Run Local Development Server

Source: https://github.com/vercel/workflow/blob/main/docs/content/docs/v5/deploying/index.mdx

Start your local development server to run workflows. No additional configuration is needed for the Local World.

```bash
npm run dev
```
